revision.comment: 飛鳥時代の人物。671年に御史大夫。壬申の乱で現れず、処罰もなし
revision.text:
'''紀大人'''（きのうし、生没年不明）は、日本の[[飛鳥時代]]の人物である。旧仮名遣いでの読みは同じ。姓（[[カバネ]]）は[[臣]]、後に[[朝臣]]。[[671年]]に御史大夫、[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。贈[[正三位]]。
天智天皇10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。
11月23日、[[大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば四天王が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。
天智天皇が死ぬと紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた[[壬申の乱]]で紀大人の活動について触れない。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったのに、紀大人については次の705年の『続日本紀』の記事まで何も記されない。
『続日本紀』慶雲2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条の、[[紀麻呂]]が死んだことに付随する説明に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。大人は正三位の官位が施行された[[701年]]以降、この705年までの間に死んだと推測できる。
贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたとも推測される。大人が処罰されなかった理由については、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて許されたのではないかとしたり、大海人皇子に内通していたのではないかと疑ったりする説がある。
[[Category:壬申の乱|きのうし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|きのうし]]

'''紀大人'''（きのうし、生没年不明）は、日本の[[飛鳥時代]]の人物である。旧仮名遣いでの読みは同じ。姓（[[カバネ]]）は[[臣]]、後に[[朝臣]]。[[671年]]に御史大夫、[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。贈[[正三位]]。
天智天皇10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。
11月23日、[[大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば四天王が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。
29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。
天智天皇が死ぬと紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた[[壬申の乱]]で紀大人の活動について触れない。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったのに、紀大人については次の705年の『続日本紀』の記事まで何も記されない。
『続日本紀』慶雲2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条の、[[紀麻呂]]が死んだことに付随する説明に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。大人は正三位の官位が施行された[[701年]]以降、この705年までの間に死んだと推測できる。
贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたとも推測される。大人が処罰されなかった理由については、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて許されたのではないかとしたり、大海人皇子に内通していたのではないかと疑ったりする説がある。
[[Category:壬申の乱|きのうし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|きのうし]]

'''紀大人'''（きのうし、青年不明 - 天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日?）は、日本の[[飛鳥時代]]の人物である。旧仮名遣いでの読みは同じ。姓（[[カバネ]]）は[[臣]]、後に[[朝臣]]。[[671年]]に御史大夫、[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。贈[[正三位]]。

29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。

天智天皇が死ぬと紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた[[壬申の乱]]で紀大人の活動について触れない。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったのに、紀大人については次の705年の『[[続日本紀]]』の記事まで何も記されない。
没年については「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある。<!-- 田中卓『壬申の乱とその前後』p116、1985年。原論文は「壬申の乱」『社会問題研究』4-4、1954年 -->しかしまた『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条の、[[紀麻呂]]が死んだことに付随する説明に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。この記事からは、大人は正三位の官位が施行された[[701年]]以降、この705年までの間に死んだと推測できる。
贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたとも推測される。大人が処罰されなかった理由については、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて許されたのではないかとしたり、大海人皇子に内通していたのではないかと疑ったりする説がある。
[[Category:壬申の乱|きのうし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|きのうし]] [[Category:紀氏|うし]]

'''紀 大人'''（き の うし、生年不明 - 天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日?）は、日本の[[飛鳥時代]]の人物である。旧仮名遣いでの読みは同じ。姓（[[カバネ]]）は[[臣]]、後に[[朝臣]]。[[671年]]に御史大夫、[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。贈[[正三位]]。

没年については「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある。<!-- 田中卓『壬申の乱とその前後』p116、1985年。原論文は「壬申の乱」『社会問題研究』4-4、1954年 -->しかしまた『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条の、[[紀麻呂]]が死んだことに付随する説明に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。この記事からは、大人は正三位の官位が施行された[[701年]]以降、この705年までの間に死んだと推測できる。

[[Category:壬申の乱|きのうし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|きのうし]] [[Category:紀氏|うし]]

== 系譜 == *父：紀大口 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：紀古麻呂<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条</ref> **男子：[[紀麻路]]<ref>『公卿補任』。但し同書の中でも大人の子とするのは疑問としている。</ref>（?-756） **男子：紀国益 **男子：紀飽邑<ref>「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵）（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による）</ref> **女子

[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。
== 経歴 == [[天智天皇]]10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。
11月23日、[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば[[四天王]]が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。
29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。
天智天皇が崩じると紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた[[壬申の乱]]で紀大人の活動について触れない。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったにもかかわらず、である。没年月日も不明だが、「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある<ref>田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁、122頁注22。</ref>。
『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条、[[紀麻呂]]の[[薨去]]記事に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたとも推測される。大人が処罰されなかった理由については、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて赦された、あるいは大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に内通していたのではないか<ref>田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁。</ref>、とする説がある。
== 系譜 == *父：紀大口 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：紀古麻呂<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条</ref> **男子：[[紀麻路]]<ref>『公卿補任』。但し同書の中でも大人の子とするのは疑問としている。</ref>（?-756） **男子：紀国益 **男子：紀飽邑<ref>「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵）（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による）</ref> **女子
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *田中卓「壬申の乱」、『壬申の乱とその前後』、1985年。論文初出は『社会問題研究』4巻4号、1954年。
{{DEFAULTSORT:き の うし}} [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:紀氏|うし]]

revision.comment: 『書紀集解』を注にくわえる。文の順序の入換え
revision.text:
'''紀 大人'''（き の うし、生年不明 - 天武天皇12年[[6月2日 (旧暦)|6月2日]]?（[[683年]][[7月1日]]?））は、日本の[[飛鳥時代]]の人物である。姓（[[カバネ]]）は[[臣]]のち[[朝臣]]。[[紀大口]]の子。[[官位]]は大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]]。
[[672年]]の壬申の乱のとき大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。
== 経歴 == [[天智天皇]]10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。
11月23日、[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば[[四天王]]が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。
天智天皇が崩じると紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた[[壬申の乱]]で紀大人の活動について触れない。『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条、[[紀麻呂]]の[[薨去]]記事に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったにもかかわらず、である。大人が処罰されなかった理由については、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて赦された、あるいは大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に内通していたのではないか<ref>河村秀根・益根『書紀集解』巻之二十七、臨川書店版第4冊1649頁。田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁。</ref>、とする説がある。
『続日本紀』の記載からは、「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたと推測される。没年月日は不明だが、「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある<ref>田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁、122頁注22。</ref>。
== 系譜 == *父：紀大口 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：紀古麻呂<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条</ref> **男子：[[紀麻路]]<ref>『公卿補任』。但し同書の中でも大人の子とするのは疑問としている。</ref>（?-756） **男子：紀国益 **男子：紀飽邑<ref>「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵）（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による）</ref> **女子

== 参考文献 == *[[河村秀根]]・益根『書紀集解』。[[阿部秋生]]解題、[[小島憲之]]補注、臨川書店、1969年。 *[[田中卓]]「壬申の乱」、『壬申の乱とその前後』、1985年。論文初出は『社会問題研究』4巻4号、1954年。

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 紀大人 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 天武天皇12年[[6月2日 (旧暦)|6月2日]]?（[[683年]][[7月1日]]?） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]] | 主君 = [[天智天皇]]→[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]→[[天武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[紀氏|紀]][[臣]]→[[朝臣]] | 父母 = 父：紀大口 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[紀麻呂|麻呂]]、[[紀古麻呂|古麻呂]]、[[紀麻路|麻路]]、国益、飽邑、
奈賀岐娘 | 特記事項 = }} '''紀 大人'''（き の うし）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の人物。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]のち[[朝臣]]。紀大口の子。[[官位]]は大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]]。 [[672年]]の[[壬申の乱]]の際、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。 == 経歴 == [[天智天皇]]10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。 11月23日、[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば[[四天王]]が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。 天智天皇が崩じると紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた壬申の乱で紀大人の活動について触れない。『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条、[[紀麻呂]]の薨去記事に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったにもかかわらず、である。大人が処罰されなかった理由について記す史料はないが、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて赦された、大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に寝返った河村秀根・益根『書紀集解』巻之二十七、臨川書店版第4冊1649頁。、内通していた田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁。、といった推測がある。 『続日本紀』の記載からは、「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたと推測される。没年月日は不明だが、「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁、122頁注22。。 == 系譜 == *父：紀大口 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：[[紀古麻呂]]『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条 **男子：[[紀麻路]]『公卿補任』。但し同書の中でも大人の子とするのは疑問としている。（?-756） **男子：紀国益 **男子：紀飽邑「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵）（[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による） **女子：紀奈賀岐娘 - [[中臣意美麻呂]]室 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[河村秀根]]・益根『書紀集解』。[[阿部秋生]]解題、[[小島憲之]]補注、臨川書店、1969年。 *[[田中卓]]「壬申の乱」、『壬申の乱とその前後』、1985年。論文初出は『社会問題研究』4巻4号、1954年。 {{DEFAULTSORT:き の うし}} [[Category:紀氏|うし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

奈賀岐娘（[[中臣意美麻呂]]室） | 特記事項 = }} '''紀 大人'''（き の うし）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の人物。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]のち[[朝臣]]。[[紀大口]]の子。[[官位]]は大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]]。 [[672年]]の[[壬申の乱]]の際、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。 == 経歴 == [[天智天皇]]10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。 11月23日、[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば[[四天王]]が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。 天智天皇が崩じると紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた壬申の乱で紀大人の活動について触れない。『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条、[[紀麻呂]]の薨去記事に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったにもかかわらず、である。大人が処罰されなかった理由について記す史料はないが、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて赦された、大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に寝返った河村秀根・益根『書紀集解』巻之二十七、臨川書店版第4冊1649頁。、内通していた田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁。、といった推測がある。 『続日本紀』の記載からは、「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたと推測される。没年月日は不明だが、「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁、122頁注22。。 == 系譜 == *父：紀大口 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：[[紀古麻呂]]『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条 **男子：[[紀麻路]]『公卿補任』。但し同書の中でも大人の子とするのは疑問としている。（?-756） **男子：[[紀国益]] **男子：[[紀飽邑]]「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵）（[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による） **女子：紀奈賀岐娘 - [[中臣意美麻呂]]室 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[河村秀根]]・[[河村益根|益根]]『書紀集解』。[[阿部秋生]]解題、[[小島憲之]]補注、臨川書店、1969年。 *[[田中卓]]「壬申の乱」、『壬申の乱とその前後』、1985年。論文初出は『社会問題研究』4巻4号、1954年。 {{DEFAULTSORT:き の うし}} [[Category:紀氏|うし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 紀 大人 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 天武天皇12年[[6月2日 (旧暦)|6月2日]]?（[[683年]][[7月1日]]?） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]] | 主君 = [[天智天皇]]→[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]→[[天武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[紀氏|紀]][[臣]]→[[朝臣]] | 父母 = 父：[[紀大口]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[紀麻呂|麻呂]]、[[紀古麻呂|古麻呂]]、[[紀国益|国益]]、[[紀飽邑|飽邑]]、
奈賀岐娘（[[中臣意美麻呂]]室） | 特記事項 = }} '''紀 大人'''（き の うし）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の人物。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]のち[[朝臣]]。[[紀大口]]の子。[[官位]]は大錦下・[[御史大夫]]、[[贈位|贈]][[正三位]]。 [[672年]]の[[壬申の乱]]の際、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）側の重臣だったが、乱後罰されなかった。 == 経歴 == [[天智天皇]]10年（[[671年]]）1月5日に、大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）が[[太政大臣]]に、[[蘇我赤兄]]が[[左大臣]]に、[[中臣金]]が[[右大臣]]に、[[蘇我果安]]、[[巨勢人]]、紀大人が[[御史大夫]]に任命された。 11月23日、[[弘文天皇|大友皇子]]と上記の左右大臣、御史大夫は、内裏の西殿の織物仏の前で「天皇の詔」を守ることを誓った。すなわち、大友皇子が香炉を手にして立ち、「天皇の詔を奉じる。もし違うことがあれば必ず天罰を被る」と誓った。続いて五人が順に香炉を取って立ち、「臣ら五人、殿下に従って天皇の詔を報じる。もし違うことがあれば[[四天王]]が打つ。天神地祇もまた罰する。三十三天、このことを証し知れ。子孫が絶え、家門必ず滅びることを」などと泣きながら誓った。ここでいう天皇の詔の内容ははっきりしないが、天智天皇の死後大友皇子を即位させることだと考えられている。29日に五人の臣は大友皇子を奉じて天智天皇の前で盟した。内容は不明だが、前の誓いと同じだと思われる。 天智天皇が崩じると紀大人は大友皇子を支える重臣になったが、『[[日本書紀]]』は続いて起きた壬申の乱で紀大人の活動について触れない。『続日本紀』[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）7月19日条、[[紀麻呂]]の薨去記事に、「近江朝の御史大夫贈正三位大人の子」とある。贈位を受けたことからみて、大人は罪人と扱われていなかったと考えられる。蘇我果安と大友皇子が自殺し、乱後の処分で中臣金が死刑、蘇我赤兄と巨勢比等（巨勢人）が流刑となったにもかかわらず、である。大人が処罰されなかった理由について記す史料はないが、同族の[[紀阿閉麻呂]]の活躍に免じて赦された、大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に寝返った河村秀根・益根『書紀集解』巻之二十七、臨川書店版第4冊1649頁。、内通していた田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁。、といった推測がある。 『続日本紀』の記載からは、「近江朝の御史大夫」を最後に公務から退いたと推測される。没年月日は不明だが、「紀氏系図」に天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）6月2日とある田中卓「壬申の乱」116頁、122頁注22。。 == 系譜 == *父：紀大口『寛政重脩諸家譜』巻第六百四十三[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445]」 *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：[[紀麻呂]]（659-705） **男子：[[紀古麻呂]]『続日本紀』天平宝字6年7月19日条 **男子：[[紀国益]] **男子：[[紀飽邑]]「中里氏系」（『宇都宮神官系図』所収、東大史料編纂所蔵） **女子：紀奈賀岐娘 - [[中臣意美麻呂]]室 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[河村秀根]]・[[河村益根|益根]]『書紀集解』。[[阿部秋生]]解題、[[小島憲之]]補注、臨川書店、1969年。 *[[田中卓]]「壬申の乱」、『壬申の乱とその前後』、1985年。論文初出は『社会問題研究』4巻4号、1954年。 {{DEFAULTSORT:き の うし}} [[Category:紀氏|うし]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[category:680年代没]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

In the Nara period, {{ill|Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ja]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:紀大人]] [[ja:v]] 2025

{{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ''' Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ''' Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ''' Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ''' Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court.
