{{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year }}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year }}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year }}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year }}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year }}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year }}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year }}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year }}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year }}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year }}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year }}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year }}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year }}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日|12=draft}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位|12=draft}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=draft}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=draft}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year|12=draft}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year|12=draft}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣|12=draft}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=draft}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=draft}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|12=draft}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=draft}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|12=draft}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=draft}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year|12=draft}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=draft}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=draft}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=draft}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=draft}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=draft}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=draft}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=draft}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=draft}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=draft}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|12=draft}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|12=draft}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame

{{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日|12=draft}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}, {{ill|posthumous rank|ja|贈位|12=draft}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=draft}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=draft}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}}. His rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|12=draft}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously awarded the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year|12=draft}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year|12=draft}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇|12=draft}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣|12=draft}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=draft}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=draft}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|12=draft}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=draft}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|12=draft}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=draft}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year|12=draft}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=draft}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=draft}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=draft}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=draft}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=draft}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=draft}}. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705th year|12=draft}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=draft}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=draft}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=draft}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=draft}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=draft}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=draft}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=draft}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=draft}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|12=draft}}) [[June 2 (lunar calendar)|June 2]]?
