{{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1|qq=}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}} of {{ill|672|ja|672nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/672|qq=}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/671|qq=}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōbun|qq=}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-daijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udaijin|qq=}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/705|qq=}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/705|qq=}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1|qq=}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/672|qq=}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} ({{ill|671|ja|671st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/671|qq=}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōbun|qq=}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-daijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udaijin|qq=}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/705|qq=}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ({{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1|qq=}}?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} → {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}. His hereditary title was initially {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, later changed to {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}} of {{ill|672|ja|672年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/672|qq=}}, he served as a senior retainer on the side of {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} ({{ill|671|ja|671年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/671|qq=}}), on January 5, {{ill|Prince Ōtomo|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōtomo|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|ja|弘文天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōbun|qq=}}) was appointed {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-daijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed {{ill|Udaijin|ja|右大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udaijin|qq=}}; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}} and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}, an entry dated {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiun|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|705|ja|705年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/705|qq=}}) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ({{ill|683|ja|683年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/683|qq=}}) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|qq=}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|qq=}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|qq=}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|qq=}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|qq=}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|qq=}}. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|qq=}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame

{{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|qq=}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|qq=}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|qq=}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|qq=}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|qq=}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame
{{Efn2|Concubine of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Ōtomo During the reign of {{ill]], {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|ja|**紀大人**|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōtoko|qq=}} served as the {{ill|Ōtsukasa|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsukasa|qq=}}. In the Nara period, {{ill|Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 2025 {{translated page|ja|紀大人|version=91138520|comment=Imported full ja history}}qq=simple|qq=User:Immanuelle/Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 |translated page|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:Asuka period people]] [[Category:Jinshin War]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:680s deaths]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:紀大人]] [[sn:Ki no Ōtoko]] [[ja:v]]

The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|qq=}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|qq=}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|qq=}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. In the Nara period, {{ill|Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 2025 {{translated page|ja|紀大人|version=91138520|comment=Imported full ja history}}qq=simple|qq=User:Immanuelle/Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 |translated page|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:Asuka period people]] [[Category:Jinshin War]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:680s deaths]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:紀大人]] [[sn:Ki no Ōtoko]] [[ja:v]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]]

The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|qq=}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|qq=}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|qq=}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|qq=}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|qq=}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|qq=}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|qq=}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|qq=}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|qq=}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|qq=}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|qq=}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|qq=}}.
