[[ja:紀大人]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|7|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605127}}; {{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605129}}; {{ill|Q106155357|WD=Q106155357}}; {{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q27920565|WD=Q27920565}}|father={{ill|Q98083044|WD=Q98083044}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame

[[ja:紀大人]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|7|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605127}}; {{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605129}}; {{ill|Q106155357|WD=Q106155357}}; {{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q27920565|WD=Q27920565}}|father={{ill|Q98083044|WD=Q98083044}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame

([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame

[[ja:紀大人]] [[ja:紀大人]] [[ja:紀大人]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|7|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605127}}; {{ill|Ki no Maro|WD=Q11605129}}; {{ill|Q106155357|WD=Q106155357}}; {{ill|Q106155725|WD=Q106155725}}; {{ill|Q27920565|WD=Q27920565}}|father={{ill|Q98083044|WD=Q98083044}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605010]] Wikidata name: Ki no Ushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ki no Ushi (紀 大人) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) [[June 2 (Lunisolar)|June 2]]? ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. ([[:en:July 1|July 1]]?) | alias = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379040}} {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] → {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} | clan = {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later [[:en:Ason|Ason]]) | parents = Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}}, {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}, {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
Nagate no Musume (wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}}) | notable_works = }} '''Ki no Ushi''' (紀 大人) was a figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His hereditary title was initially [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], later changed to [[:en:Ason|Ason]]. He was the son of {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}. His Rank was {{ill|Dai Kinshita|ja|大錦下|1=Dai Kinge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Kinshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439798}}・{{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}, and he was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of [[:en:672|672]], he served as a senior retainer on the side of [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] but was not punished after the conflict. == Career == In the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] ([[:en:671|671]]), on January 5, [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Prince Ōtomo]] (later [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kōbun]]) was appointed [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō-daijin]]; {{ill|Soga no Akae|ja|蘇我赤兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Akae|1=Soga no Akae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625208}} became [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]]; {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} was appointed [[:en:Minister of the Right|Udaijin]]; and {{ill|Soga no Kayasu|ja|蘇我果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kayasu|1=Hatayasu Soga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625201}}, {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}}, and Ki no Ushi were appointed {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. On November 23, Prince Ōtomo, along with the aforementioned ministers, swore to uphold the "Emperor's decree" in the western palace hall before a Buddha image woven from textiles. Prince Ōtomo took the incense burner, declaring, "I uphold the Emperor's decree. If I deviate from it, I shall be punished by divine retribution." Following this, the five ministers each swore to support the decree, invoking the protection of the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]] and the divine punishment of the {{ill|kami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt_ja=天神地祇|lt=Tenjin Chigi}} if they betrayed the oath. The specifics of the "Emperor's decree" are unclear, but it is believed to have involved the succession of Prince Ōtomo after [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s passing. On the 29th, they reaffirmed this oath before Emperor Tenji, likely repeating the earlier pledge. After Emperor Tenji's death, Ki no Ushi became a key retainer supporting Prince Ōtomo. However, the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] does not mention his activities during the ensuing Jinshin War. According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], an entry dated [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 2 ([[:en:705|705]]) mentions that {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} was the "son of Ki no Ushi, {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court, posthumously awarded {{ill|Jushii|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}." This suggests that Ushi was not treated as a criminal after the war, unlike others such as Soga no Kayasu and Prince Ōtomo, who committed suicide, and Nakatomi no Kane, who was executed. Soga no Akae and Kose no Hito were exiled. The reasons for Ki no Ushi's pardon are unknown, but hypotheses include intervention by his kinsman {{ill|Ki no Abe no Maro|ja|紀阿閉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Abe no Maro|1=Ki no Abe no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605114}}, defection to {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), or secret negotiations. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] implies that he retired from public service after his tenure as {{ill|Omatsurigoto no Futo|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsurigoto no Futo|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}} of the Ōmi Court. His death date is uncertain, but genealogical records indicate he died on Tenmu 12 ([[:en:683|683]]) {{ill|June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (Lunisolar)|lt=June 2 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月2日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Ki no Ōguchi|ja|紀大口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōguchi|1=Ki no Ōkuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98083044}}{{Cite web|title=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu Vol. 643|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2577445}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605127}} (659–705) ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Komaro|ja|紀古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Komaro|1=Ki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920565}}[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] Tenpyō Hōji 6, July 19 ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Kunikoku|ja|紀国益|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kunikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Son: {{ill|Ki no Akui|ja|紀飽邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Akui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Found in "Nakazato-shi Kei" (included in {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja|宇都宮神官系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine genealogy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, held by the University of Tokyo Historical Compilation Institute) ** Daughter: {{ill|Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja|紀奈賀岐娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Nagate no Musume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Wife of {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366913}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{ill|Kawamura Hidenari|ja|河村秀根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Hidenari|1=Kawamura Hidene|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536032}}, {{ill|Kawamura Masunari|ja|益根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamura Masunari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ''Shoki Shūge''. Edited by {{ill|Abe Akiyuki|ja|阿部秋生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Akiyuki|1=Akio Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657912}}, supplementary notes by {{ill|Kojima Noriyuki|ja|小島憲之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Noriyuki|1=Noriyuki Kojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460159}}, Rinsen Shoten, 1969. * {{ill|Tanaka Taku|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Taku|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}. "The Jinshin War," in ''The Jinshin War and its Aftermath''. 1985. Originally published in ''Social Problems Research,'' Vol. 4, No. 4, 1954. * [[Tajihi no Shima]] * Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōtoko|lt=Ki no Ōhito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōhito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ...5 KB (661 words) - 06:00, 23 2025 * [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{familytree |border=0| 001 | 002 | 003 | 004 |001={{ill|Ki Ōbito|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Ōbito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|002=Makoto|003=Yumihari|004=Ononame
{{Efn2|Concubine of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Ōtomo During the reign of {{ill]], {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|ja|**紀大人**|1=Ki no Ushi|2=ja|3=紀大人|WD=Q11605010|lt='''Ki no Ushi'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōtoko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the {{ill|Ōtsukasa|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsukasa|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071155}}. In the Nara period, {{ill|Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 2025 {{translated page|ja|紀大人|version=91138520|comment=Imported full ja history}}qq=simple|qq=User:Immanuelle/Ki no M ... 27 KB (3,326 words) - 02:59, 23 |translated page|qq=}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:紀大人]] [[ja:v]] {{wikidata link|Q11605010}} [[Category:680s deaths]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Jinshin War]] [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
